Key insight: GUARD_TRUTHY must work on raw JS booleans from comparisons, not MonkeyObjects. 5 new tests, all 157 existing tests still pass. End-to-end: counter 0→100 via compiled trace works.

blocks.summary: Fixed 3 JIT integration bugs: (1) CONST_INT typeMap was 'int' not 'raw_int' — caused…
blocks.details: Fixed 3 JIT integration bugs: (1) CONST_INT typeMap was 'int' not 'raw_int' — caused UNBOX_INT to call .value on raw numbers → NaN; (2) UNBOX_INT emitted redundantly for already-raw values; (3) GUARD_TRUTHY used JS truthiness on MonkeyBoolean objects (always truthy as objects). All 7 integration + 157 existing tests pass. Pattern: type confusion at raw/boxed boundary is the #1 JIT bug class.

blocks.task: Blog: finish + polish + publish
blocks.summary: JIT opcode coverage expansion.
blocks.details: JIT opcode coverage expansion. Added recording for superinstructions (OpGetLocal*Const → decompose to LOAD_LOCAL+CONST_INT+arith IR), OpGetFree, OpBang. 7.8x speedup on 10k loop benchmark. 166 tests passing (9 JIT integration tests).

blocks.task: Blog: finish + polish + publish
blocks.summary: JIT trace optimization passes.
blocks.details: JIT trace optimization passes. Added TraceOptimizer with 3 passes (redundant guard elimination, constant folding, DCE). Fixed critical compaction bug — _compact() was remapping value/slot/index operands as IR refs, corrupting constants. 5 new tests, 171 total passing. Hot loop: 6.4x JIT vs VM.

blocks.details: Design review. Way ahead of schedule — scaffold through optimizer done by 09:30 (was planned through 11:30). blocks.task: Fix optimizer regression (7.8x→6.4x), investigate
blocks.summary: JIT loop variable promotion.
blocks.details: JIT loop variable promotion. Detected loop-carried globals (STORE with BOX_INT), promoted to raw JS `let` vars before loop, write-back on exit via `__wb()`. Eliminated box/unbox per iteration. Also: GUARD_INT skip for promoted vars, dead BOX_INT elision, raw bool guard shortcut. Hot loop 7.2x→12.5x. 171 tests pass.

blocks.task: Fix optimizer regression (7.8x→6.4x), investigate

blocks.details: Commit verified (loop variable promotion already pushed). Dashboard + daily log update.

blocks.task: Side trace architecture design
blocks.summary: Side trace architecture design.
blocks.details: Side trace architecture design. Decided on VM-dispatched approach (parent exit checks sideTraces map, not code patching). Wrote scratch note with full design. ~100 lines to implement. tracing-jit-compilation note at 4 uses — due for formal promotion. Schedule on track.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: side traces (guard exit → new trace recording) (est: 6 blocks)
blocks.summary: Implemented side traces.
blocks.details: Implemented side traces. HOT_EXIT_THRESHOLD=8, MAX_SIDE_TRACES=4 per root. TraceRecorder.startSideTrace() records from exitIp to parent loop header. VM dispatches side traces on guard failure, loop_back re-enters parent. 2 new integration tests. 173/173 pass. Diagnostic shows side trace firing 91x in branching loop. Hot loop still 12.2x.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: side traces (guard exit → new trace recording) (est: 6 blocks)
blocks.summary: Function inlining in tracing JIT.
blocks.details: Function inlining in tracing JIT. Trace recorder now follows execution into called functions (MAX_INLINE_DEPTH=3). Key design: inlineSlotRefs map passes caller's arg IR refs directly to callee's LOAD_LOCAL (avoids STORE_LOCAL + promotion conflicts). Fixed BOX_INT dead-code elision bug — wasn't checking non-store uses (UNBOX_INT from inlined return values). Fixed trace exit IP handling for max_iter/call exits. 175/175 tests (2 new). Function calls in loop: 14.68x vs VM. Hot loop: 13.81x.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: side traces (guard exit → new trace recording) (est: 6 blocks)
blocks.summary: Fixed 3 JIT bugs, created comprehensive benchmark suite.
blocks.details: Fixed 3 JIT bugs, created comprehensive benchmark suite. (1) Non-promoted STORE_GLOBAL/STORE_LOCAL stored raw ints instead of MonkeyInteger — broke nested loops (side trace set j=0 as raw, inner trace read .value→undefined). (2) Removed `| 0` int32 truncation from compiled arithmetic — 100k sum was wrapping. (3) Converted recursive _executeTrace to iterative — eliminated stack overflow on large side-traced loops. 177/177 tests. Highlights: 21.5x hot loop, 15.9x 100k sum, 5.5x nested, 6.7x inlined. Known bug: inlined fn guards + side trace recording → invalid IP exit.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: side traces (guard exit → new trace recording) (est: 6 blocks)
blocks.summary: Fixed inlined function guard exit IP bug.
blocks.details: Fixed inlined function guard exit IP bug. Root cause: guards inside inlined functions exited to trace.startIp (loop header) instead of the call site. Fix: getGuardExitIp() now returns outermost callSiteIp; all type guards (GUARD_INT/BOOL/STRING) store per-guard exitIp during recording. 178/178 tests. Benchmarks stable.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: side traces (guard exit → new trace recording) (est: 6 blocks)
blocks.summary: Trace stitching framework + nested loop analysis.
blocks.details: Trace stitching framework + nested loop analysis.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: side traces (guard exit → new trace recording) (est: 6 blocks)
blocks.summary: All repos clean — JIT work committed through trace stitching (03ac40e).
blocks.details: All repos clean — JIT work committed through trace stitching (03ac40e). Blog, webread, workspace all pushed. Dashboard updated. Added EXEC_TRACE IR opcode for calling inner compiled traces during outer trace execution. Key discovery: nested loops already work via side traces — inner loop is root trace, outer loop becomes a side trace off the inner trace's guard exit. The 4.5x nested perf is bottlenecked by per-iteration function call overhead in side trace dispatch (14-arg JS function call per outer iteration). Cached allConsts in dispatch path. Next: inline side trace code in root trace guard exits to eliminate dispatch overhead. 178/178 tests.

blocks.task: Commit side trace progress
Review progress, replan (inlining done early)
blocks.summary: Way ahead of schedule — inlining+side traces done 3h early (was planned through 14:30).
blocks.details: Way ahead of schedule — inlining+side traces done 3h early (was planned through 14:30). Reprioritized: inline side trace code into root guard exits to eliminate dispatch overhead (nested 5.5x→target 10x+), then fib tracing+benchmarks. tracing-jit scratch note at 4 uses — due for promotion at 14:45. Updated SCHEDULE.md.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: inline side trace code into root guards (eliminate dispatch overhead)
blocks.summary: Inlined side trace dispatch into compiled root traces.
blocks.details: Inlined side trace dispatch into compiled root traces. Added __sideTraces parameter to compiled functions; guard exits now check for side traces inline and call them directly (no VM roundtrip). On loop_back, root trace continues via 'continue loop'. Added __reloadPromoted() for re-reading promoted vars after side trace modifies globals/locals. All 178 tests passing. Benchmarks: hot loop 20.3x, 100k sum 13.7x, nested loops 4.2x, inlined fns 5-8x. The nested loop improvement is modest (4.2x) because side traces are still separate compiled functions — true IR-level inlining would merge them.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: inline side trace code into root guards (eliminate dispatch overhead)
blocks.summary: Recursive function tracing — implemented FunctionCompiler (method JIT for self-recursive functions).
blocks.details: Recursive function tracing — implemented FunctionCompiler (method JIT for self-recursive functions). Two compilation modes: boxed (generic) and raw integer (specialized). Raw mode generates inner __rawFib that works entirely with raw JS numbers — zero boxing at recursion boundaries. Fixed _canUseRawInts to only check referenced constants (not entire pool, which includes CompiledFunction objects). Added storeFuncTrace, _executeFuncTrace, hot function counting (threshold 16). 178/178 tests. fib(25): 10.4x JIT/VM (118ms→11.36ms), 14x JIT/Eval. Key architectural insight: the inner raw function calls itself directly — boxing only happens at the VM↔JIT boundary, not at each recursion level. This is effectively what LuaJIT does with type-specialized traces.

Added 3 stress benchmarks: fib(30) 18.3x vs eval/11.8x vs VM, loop+fib 10.1x, factorial(20). Fixed expected values for nested fn calls and conditional fn benchmarks. All 185 tests passing, all benchmarks correct.

Discovered JIT closure bug: free variable closures crash with "unknown opcode: 0" after trace compilation triggers. Arrays show 0 traces (JIT overhead without benefit). Higher-order fns excellent (11-16x). Lexer note: identifiers can't contain digits (add5→add + 5).

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: comprehensive benchmarks + fib tracing
blocks.summary: Fixed closure free variable JIT crash.
blocks.details: Fixed closure free variable JIT crash. Two bugs: (1) LOAD_FREE in inlined closures referenced root __free array instead of callee closure — fixed by emitting captured values as constants (Monkey captures by value). (2) Guard exit IP for inlined fns pointed to OpCall operand byte, not instruction start — caused VM to misinterpret operand as opcode. Added 2 integration tests. 187/187 pass. Closure benchmarks: adder 8.7x, multiplier 7.2x.

blocks.task: Monkey JIT: comprehensive benchmarks + fib tracing
blocks.summary: Trace abort blacklist.
blocks.details: Trace abort blacklist. After 3 failed recording attempts at a back-edge, blacklist it (like LuaJIT's penalty system). Eliminated JIT overhead for untraceable code (arrays, hashes, builtins). Arrays: 0.5x→1.0x, hashes: 0.8x stable. Also refactored all abort sites to use _abortRecording() helper. 187/187 tests. Nested loops improved 4.2x→5.7x.

blocks.task: Higher-order consciousness theories (HOT, HOROR)
blocks.summary: Higher-Order Theories of consciousness deep dive.
blocks.details: Higher-Order Theories of consciousness deep dive. Researched Rosenthal's HOT (actualist — conscious state requires actual concurrent higher-order thought), Carruthers' DHOT/HOROR (dispositional — availability to HOT suffices, dual-content self-representation), Lycan's HOP (inner sense, now abandoned), and Lau's PRM (perceptual reality monitoring — PFC determines if percept is "real"). Created comprehensive scratch note (consciousness-hot.md). Key insight: PRM is the most scientifically rigorous modern HOT variant but faces same PFC challenges as GNW after COGITATE. Updated consciousness-index.md and comparison notes. Credence: HOT as complete theory 15%, HOT-style monitoring as one component 50%.

blocks.task: Continue: HOT vs AST vs IIT comparison
blocks.summary: Integrated HOT/PRM as 5th peer theory into consciousness-comparison.md.
blocks.details: Integrated HOT/PRM as 5th peer theory into consciousness-comparison.md. Added HOT across all 6 dimensions: COGITATE analysis (PFC failure challenges HOT similarly to GNW), explanatory power (HOT wins conscious/unconscious divide), AI implications (metacognition requirement — middle of optimism spectrum), weaknesses, divergent predictions, conflict table. Key insight: HOT and GNW share PFC vulnerability but HOT has narrower escape route (monitoring ≠ re-representation). Updated credences.

blocks.task: Tracing JIT: LuaJIT trace exit handling + side traces
blocks.summary: LuaJIT trace exit mechanics deep dive.
blocks.details: LuaJIT trace exit mechanics deep dive. Read lj_asm_x86.h (exit stub generation), lj_snap.c (snapshot restore), lj_trace.c (trace_hotside + lj_asm_patchexit). Documented full x86 exit path: guard fail → exit stub (2 bytes: push exitno + jmp) → group handler → lj_vm_exit_handler → save ExitState → lj_snap_restore (walk snapshot entries, restore stack slots, skip NORESTORE) → trace_hotside (count exits, trigger side trace at threshold 10) → lj_asm_patchexit (scan parent mcode, rewrite jcc targets to side trace mcode). Added "Trace Exit Mechanics" section to tracing-jit-compilation.md.

blocks.task: Copy-and-patch deep dive: stencil extraction details
blocks.summary: Copy-and-patch stencil extraction deep dive.
blocks.details: Copy-and-patch stencil extraction deep dive. Read CPython Tools/jit/ source (_targets.py, _stencils.py). Documented full pipeline: per-uop Clang compilation with -Os/-fno-builtin, assembly optimizer pass, llvm-readobj JSON extraction, Hole/relocation mapping (26 relocation types across x86_64/AArch64/i686), GOT elimination at build time, AArch64 ADRP+LDR fold optimization, async parallel build via TaskGroup. Key insight: copy-and-patch is literally a runtime linker — object file relocations replayed at JIT time. Updated scratch note to uses:2.

blocks.task: GraalVM/Truffle: partial evaluation + Futamura projection
blocks.summary: GraalVM/Truffle PE deep dive.
blocks.details: GraalVM/Truffle PE deep dive. Added: PE algorithm mechanics (aggressive inlining of compilation-constant dispatches + constant folding cascade = interpreter elimination), all three Futamura projections with practical comparison (Truffle = 1st projection at runtime, PyPy ≈ 2nd projection at build time), sea-of-nodes internals (fixed vs floating nodes, automatic LICM via scheduling, GVN via structural identity, FrameState for cheap deopt), full JIT pipeline (7 stages from AST to machine code), Bytecode DSL (post-22.1 warmup fix). Updated scratch note to uses:2.
